blocks.content: They stopped at the corner without deciding to.
Magnolia left, his street right. Kit put his foot down on the pavement and stood there with the bike between his hands.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Promise me you make it out.
A long moment. The sprinklers on the corner made their arc. A car turned off a block away.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Some things are better when you don't forget them.
She said it quietly and turned onto Magnolia without looking back.
Kit stood at the corner and watched her go.

Upstairs, with the ceiling fan going and the heat pressing through the screen, he lay on his bed and thought about the child's drawing on the wall of the Henderson house. Daisy's shoulder against his arm in the dark, steady as anything.
Some things are better when you don't forget them.
He wasn't going to forget any of it.
